steps.title: Connect the discriminant to the number of solutions
steps.content:
The question asks for the number of distinct real solutions. For a quadratic, that means checking whether the graph crosses the $x$-axis twice, touches it once, or never reaches it. The discriminant $b^2-4ac$ gives that count. For $4x^2+12x+17=0$:
$$b^2-4ac=12^2-4(4)(17)=144-272=-128$$ A negative discriminant means the parabola never crosses the $x$-axis, so the equation has no real solutions.

steps.title: Choose the answer
steps.content: The discriminant is negative, so the equation has $0$ distinct real solutions. D is correct.
